Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Continued To Apr-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Paul Sachs (Trustee).

The Court stated that before it was the first account, request for fees, and reduction of bond. The Court addressed its concern with regard to the allocations/split at 47% for Miranda and %53 for Lena. In addition, the Court questioned why the split could not be 50/50. Ms. Ng stated that this was a lengthy administration, that it all comes down to percentages, and explained how they arrived at the 47/53 split. Discussions about the income/allocations were held. The Court proposed a "cleaner" way to allocate the funds stating that the income could be split at 50/50, that the expenses could be split 50/50, and that then the additional expenses with regard to Miranda could be taken out of her allocation. Additional discussions were held. Ms. Ng requested one month to prepare the amended accounting. The Court continued the matter to April 4, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of the amended account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Paul Sachs (Executor).

Ms. Ng requested additional time to prepare and file the petition stating that they needed more time to present the terms of the trust, that two of the beneficiaries are minors, and that the estate only owned seventy-five percent of the propery. The Court inquired as to the provisions of the trust, if the funds were properly invested, and advised Ms. Ng that it might be a good idea to have the terms ready when the petition is filed. Ms. Ng informed the Court that the funds were properly invested. The matter was continued to March 3, 2009,

Judge: Raymond Arata Jr. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
